items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Three days ago, when Helios was launched, we registered an unexpected spike in Voidmatter concentrations near Hyvatia. It interfered with the launch.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: We're still unsure what triggered the surge. But after comparing the waveforms with the device Professor Mornye brought back, we suspect it was an artificially induced Void Storm.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: And we found you unconscious close by.

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Dr. Herssen, I really can't remember anything...

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: (Chuckles) You can relax. Professor Lucilla has already given you the clear. You're not a suspect.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: I'm just here for protective oversight. Acting as your personal bodyguard, you might say.

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Aren't you the campus doctor? And you're... here to protect me?

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Heh, a doctor's duty, isn't it? But there is one thing I need your testimony on.

items.speaker: Luuk Herssen
items.text: Before you fainted, who did you see?

items.speaker: Researcher?
items.text: Good day, gentlemen! Hyvatia's routine maintenance has been completed. I'll be on my way.

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Who's that?

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Never mind. No time to think about that.

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Dr. Herssen, the Academy's review committee thinks what I've said is unbelievable. But if you're willing to trust me...

items.speaker: Anslem
items.text: Before I passed out, I saw... myself.
Ah, it seems my hastily chosen pawn has proven to be utterly ineffectual. It appears I'll have to add the final flourish to push the story to its climax.
But no matter, no matter. I'll simply chalk this up as a poor judgment call. A band of fools who enacted a farce.
Such a pity, though. With this piece expended, the chapter where the girl forms her bond with the Threnodian must be delayed.
But that is a tale to be savored later. Right, {PlayerName}?

dialog.stepLabel: [A] After the fight with the Reactor Drive Overlord

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Please wait, don't come in yet—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Ugh... Ahh!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Sorry, my body... I still can't move like I normally do.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I've gone through rehab before, and it's been a while since this happened. Don't worry, I can handle this much on my own. Ugh...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Professor Mornye, how are you feeling now?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Dr. Herssen said you were in bad shape. It's great to see you've recovered a little.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: To be honest, at that time, I didn't think I'd make it.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But... but when I was drifting in and out of consciousness, I thought I saw a girl with a light blue ring.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: A light blue ring?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Can you describe what you saw in more detail?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I... I can't be sure, but I think she protected me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (It sounds like the girl Mornye saw is the same one I encountered when I first got to Lahai-Roi.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (She's able to go up against the Threnodian herself, and appear in the Reactor Drive. She's connected with the Exostrider and the Threnodian in ways we still don't fully understand.)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: (Figuring out who she is and what happened to her will be the next big task.)

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: {PlayerName}? {PlayerName}—Hey, stop spacing out!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: To celebrate Professor Mornye waking up and the crisis being averted, don't you think we should...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Go for a ride!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Nothing beats the wind on your face, especially when—

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: The three of us still don't have a group picture!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Heh, a photo under the new Helios? Sounds like it'll make a good souvenir.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Witnessing the rise of the new sun together. That's also the promise between the two of us, Mentor.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: So? Not bad, huh?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Back in the day, whenever I was stressed about the future, hanging out here always made me feel a little better.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Look, now we can just enjoy the breeze without a care in the world!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I used to think that when I saw this view, I'd cry because my wish would be fulfilled.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But now, when I see Helios's light pouring over the land, with the ancient frostlands and people who've been here for only a few decades sharing the same warmth...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I just think, what a beautiful sight.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Well, let's scream that from the rooftops!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: To freedom!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: To civilization.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: To new beginnings.
